# cv-web

A personal CV website with a Typst-generated PDF — same data, multiple layouts, fully configurable styles.

**Live site** renders the CV as a Next.js web page. **PDF** is generated by Typst at build time and served as a vector file with working hyperlinks (`mailto:`, `tel:`, internal anchors).

---

## How it works

Three concerns, three places:

| What | Where | Controls |
|---|---|---|
| **Content** | `src/data/cv.json` | Everything you say — experience, skills, education… |
| **Styles** | `src/typst/tokens.typ` + `theme.typ` | Fonts, colours, spacing scale |
| **Layout** | `src/layouts/*.json` | Section order, columns, header style |

Change your data → regenerate. Change a token → every size/gap updates proportionally. Swap a layout file → same data, different arrangement.

---

## Quick start

### 1. Clone and install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/you/cv-web
cd cv-web
npm install
```

### 2. Add your data

```bash
cp src/data/cv.example.json src/data/cv.json
```

Edit `src/data/cv.json` with your real information. The file is gitignored — it stays on your machine.

### 3. Generate the PDF

```bash
npm run generate-pdf
# or with a specific layout:
bash scripts/generate-pdf.sh classic
```

Typst is downloaded automatically on first run (~10 MB, cached in `/tmp`).

### 4. Run locally

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

---

## Layouts

Layouts live in `src/layouts/`. Each is a JSON file describing section order and arrangement — no code changes needed to switch.

```bash
bash scripts/generate-pdf.sh            # default layout → public/cv.pdf
bash scripts/generate-pdf.sh classic    # classic layout → public/cv-classic.pdf
bash scripts/generate-pdf.sh alt        # alt layout     → public/cv-alt.pdf
```

### Creating a new layout

Copy an existing layout and edit:

```bash
cp src/layouts/default.json src/layouts/my-layout.json
bash scripts/generate-pdf.sh my-layout
```

**Layout properties:**

```json
{
  "header": {
    "style": "split"
  },
  "sections": [
    { "id": "summary",    "breakable": true  },
    { "id": "experience", "breakable": true  },
    {
      "type": "columns",
      "columns": 2,
      "content": [
        ["education", "languages"],
        ["certifications"]
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

| Property | Values | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `header.style` | `"split"`, `"stacked"` | Name+headline left / contact right, or all stacked |
| `id` | section name | Which section to render |
| `breakable` | `true` / `false` | Allow page breaks inside this section |
| `type` | `"full"` (default), `"columns"` | Full width or multi-column |
| `columns` | `2`, `3`, `4` | Column count (gutter auto-selected from theme) |
| `content` | array of arrays | Which sections go in each column |

Available section ids: `summary`, `experience`, `awards`, `skills`, `education`, `languages`, `certifications`, `side_projects`.

---

## Customising styles

All visual constants are in `src/typst/tokens.typ`. Four root knobs control everything:

```typst
#let fs-base       = 8pt      // scale all font sizes
#let sp-unit       = 0.5em    // scale all spacing
#let margin-base   = 1.1cm    // scale all margins
#let tracking-base = 1pt      // scale all letter-spacing
```

Semantic aliases in `src/typst/theme.typ` map tokens to named roles (`section-pre`, `c-link`, `col-gutter-2`, etc.) — edit those to remap specific elements without touching the scale.

---

## Deployment (Vercel)

Push to GitHub and connect to Vercel. The `prebuild` npm hook runs Typst automatically before `next build` — the PDF is generated as part of the build, no separate step needed.

```
git push origin main   # Vercel picks up the push, runs prebuild, deploys
```

The PDF ends up at `/cv.pdf` on your deployed site.

---

## Keeping your data private

This repo ships with `src/data/cv.json` gitignored. The recommended setup:

1. **Public repo** (this template) — code, layouts, styles, example data
2. **Private repo** (your fork) — same code + your real `cv.json`

To pull template updates into your private repo:

```bash
git remote add template https://github.com/you/cv-web
git fetch template
git merge template/main
```

Only `cv.json` will conflict (your data vs the example) — everything else updates cleanly.

---

## File structure

```
src/
  data/
    cv.example.json     ← copy this to cv.json and fill in your data
    cv.json             ← gitignored — your private data goes here
  typst/
    cv.typ              ← entry point: page setup, header, layout loop
    tokens.typ          ← root knobs + all derived scales
    theme.typ           ← semantic aliases + colours
    components.typ      ← cv-section divider, pill, separator
    sections.typ        ← render functions for each section
  layouts/
    default.json        ← split header, 2-column edu+lang / certs
    classic.json        ← stacked header, all sections full-width
    alt.json            ← split header, 2-column edu+lang / skills
  components/           ← Next.js React components (web view)
  app/                  ← Next.js app router
scripts/
  generate-pdf.sh       ← downloads Typst, compiles cv.typ
public/
  *.pdf                 ← gitignored — generated at build time
```

---

## Requirements

- Node.js 18+
- Internet access on first PDF generation (downloads Typst ~10 MB, cached)
- No other local dependencies
